cards.name: The Bridge Keeper
cards.profile: Guards the crossing between JavaScript and native. Knows what truly happens when a call leaves the JS thread, and charges a toll in complexity. Speaks fluent Objective-C and Kotlin, and will gently remind you the New Architecture changed everything.

cards.name: The Yak Shaver
cards.profile: Set out to change one button's color. Three days later is patching a transitive dependency's Gradle config to fix the linter that broke the formatter. Has forgotten the original task. Is, somehow, having a wonderful time.

cards.name: The Expo Evangelist
cards.profile: Has seen the promised land and it has no Xcode. Will tell you, unprompted, that you don't need to eject anymore. Radiates the deep calm of someone whose builds are simply handled. Possibly correct.

cards.name: The Demo Gods' Supplicant
cards.profile: Makes offerings before every live demo. Knows the wifi will betray them, the simulator will freeze, and the one untested path is the one they'll show. Prays anyway. Demos anyway. This is faith.

cards.name: The Refactor Mystic
cards.profile: Sees the truer architecture shimmering beneath the code. Cannot ship until the structure is correct. The feature works — that is not the point. The feature is not yet beautiful.

cards.name: The Offline-First Monk
cards.profile: Builds for the subway, the basement, the rural two-lane road. Assumes the network is a lie. Treats connectivity as a pleasant surprise rather than a right. Wholly at peace with the sync queue.

cards.name: The Hallucination Archaeologist
cards.profile: Digs through code the AI wrote with total confidence, unearthing methods that have never existed in any library, anywhere. Carries a brush and a deep sigh.

cards.name: The Vibe Coder
cards.profile: Does not read the diff. Feels the diff. Communes with the agent, accepts all, and somehow ships. Terrifying. Possibly the future.

cards.name: The Prompt Whisperer
cards.profile: Believes the right incantation unlocks the machine spirit. Says 'please' and 'you're an expert' to a language model. It... kind of works?

cards.name: The Last Artisan
cards.profile: Refuses the autocomplete. Types every character by hand, smug and unhurried. 'I like to understand my code.' Will outlive us all, or be the first against the wall.

cards.name: The Agent Wrangler
cards.profile: Has six agents running in six terminals and has lost track of which one is editing prod. A shepherd whose flock has scattered across the hills.

cards.name: The Context Hoarder
cards.profile: Pastes the entire codebase into the window every time. Treats the token limit as a personal insult. Will not, under any circumstance, be told to be selective.

cards.name: The Reanimator
cards.profile: Bends elements to their will at 60fps. Speaks in springs and shared values. Makes things slide that need not slide. Pure motion, questionable restraint.
cards.notes: Overlaps with The Gesture Handler — rank head-to-head, may merge.

cards.name: The Gesture Handler
cards.profile: Will spend a full day on the pan-and-fling so it feels right in the thumb. Speaks with their hands; the hands are always demoing a swipe. Has opinions about the rubber-band at the edge of a scroll. The animation is invisible and perfect, and it took a week.
cards.notes: Justin's pun idea: react-native-gesture-handler + physical hand gestures + the animation perfectionist.

cards.name: The Standup Ghost
cards.profile: Present in body, camera off, 'no blockers.' Has not spoken in the meeting since March. A quiet, benevolent haunting.

cards.name: The Merge Conflict Gladiator
cards.profile: Enters the arena of <<<<<<< HEAD and emerges bloodied but victorious. Has resolved conflicts they did not understand and never will. Accept incoming. Accept current. Accept fate.

cards.name: The Backwards-Compatibility Martyr
cards.profile: Suffers so that users on a 2019 Android phone may know peace. Maintains the polyfill nobody else will touch. Asks for nothing. Deserves everything.

cards.name: The Hallway Track Sage
cards.profile: Has not attended a single session. The hallway is the conference. Has had four life-changing conversations by the coffee urn and missed every talk on purpose. This is the way.
cards.notes: Conference-meta — lands harder in the room.

cards.name: The First-Question Gladiator
cards.profile: Reaches the mic before the applause fades. 'More of a comment than a question.' The room knows. The speaker knows. Asks it anyway, for all of us who never would.

cards.name: The Swag Forager
cards.profile: Has optimized the sponsor-booth loop for maximum t-shirt yield. Knows which booth restocked the good stickers. Tote bag is full by 10am. Will demo your product if it means a second water bottle.

cards.name: The Sticker Diplomat
cards.profile: Laptop lid is a treaty map of every conference attended. Trades stickers like currency, reads alliances in logos. Quietly the most connected person in the building. Soft power, hard shell.

cards.name: The TestFlight Necromancer
cards.profile: Submits the build at 11pm and refreshes App Store Connect like a slot machine. Has named all forty-seven builds. Knows the reviewer's timezone better than their own family's.

cards.name: The Type Astronaut
cards.profile: Has typed the un-typeable. The generics are correct and no living human can read them. Left orbit somewhere around the third conditional type and has not returned. The compiler is happy. Ascended.

cards.name: The Console.log Detective
cards.profile: Refuses the debugger. Solves every mystery with strategically placed print statements. 'here', 'here2', 'WHY'. Cracks the case every time and ships one of the logs to prod.
cards.notes: Universal-guilt card — everyone's guilty, so it bonds rather than stings.

cards.name: The Dependency Doomsayer
cards.profile: Runs npm audit and reads the results aloud at standup. Sees forty-seven critical vulnerabilities in a static marketing site and will not be calmed. The supply chain is coming for us all.

cards.name: The Ship-It Gremlin
cards.profile: Done is better than perfect, and also better than tested. Merges on Friday at 4:55. Lives without fear because lives without staging. The hotfix is part of the plan. The hotfix was always the plan.

cards.name: The Flag Gardener
cards.profile: Tends a sprawling garden of feature flags, half of them rolled out, none of them removed. Cannot remember which are on in prod. Waters them all the same. Something in there has gone feral.

cards.name: The Storybook Hermit
cards.profile: Built the component library in perfect isolation — documented, themed, accessible, alone. Nobody imports it. They are at peace in their workshop, polishing buttons no one will press.
